message:
chore(beads): close 8ado - Ratings E2E tests complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -109,7 +109,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-82jx","title":"Implement user following and activity feed","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7ly5. ## Task Allow users to follow creators and see activity feed. ## Why This Matters - Social engagement - Stay updated on favorite creators - Build community - Increase retention ## Features ### 1. Following System - Follow/unfollow users - Following count display - Followers count display - Following list page ### 2. Activity Feed - New prompts from followed users - New collections from followed - Updates to followed content - Filtered by type ### 3. Notifications - New follower notification - Optional email digest - Activity summary ### 4. Discovery - Suggested users to follow - Popular creators - Similar to your interests ## Database Schema ```typescript export const userFollows = pgTable('user_follows', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), followerId: uuid('follower_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), followeeId: uuid('followee_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ unique('uq_follow').on(table.followerId, table.followeeId), index('idx_follows_follower').on(table.followerId), index('idx_follows_followee').on(table.followeeId), ]); export const activityFeed = pgTable('activity_feed', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Feed owner actorId: uuid('actor_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Who did it activityType: text('activity_type').notNull(), // new_prompt, new_collection, etc. resourceType: text('resource_type'), resourceId: uuid('resource_id'), seen: boolean('seen').default(false), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ index('idx_feed_user').on(table.userId), index('idx_feed_time').on(table.createdAt), ]); ``` ## Implementation ### Follow Actions ```typescript async function followUser(followerId: string, followeeId: string) { await db.insert(userFollows).values({ followerId, followeeId }); await createNotification(followeeId, 'new_follower', { followerId }); await updateFollowCounts(followerId, followeeId); } ``` ### Feed Generation ```typescript async function getFeed(userId: string, limit = 20) { // Get activities from followed users return db.select() .from(activityFeed) .where(eq(activityFeed.userId, userId)) .orderBy(desc(activityFeed.createdAt)) .limit(limit); } ``` ### Fan-out on Write When user creates content: 1. Get all followers 2. Insert feed entry for each follower 3. Batch insert for efficiency ## UI Components - FollowButton - Follow/unfollow - FollowStats - Follower/following counts - ActivityFeed - Scrollable feed - SuggestedUsers - Users to follow ## Files to Create - src/lib/db/schema/follows.ts - src/lib/social/follows.ts - src/lib/social/feed.ts - src/app/api/users/[id]/follow/route.ts - src/app/(app)/feed/page.tsx - src/components/social/follow-button.tsx ## Success Criteria - Following works correctly - Feed shows relevant activity - Counts accurate - Performance acceptable ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:19:00.684856040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T02:06:43.961916218Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T02:06:43.961916218Z","close_reason":"Implemented following schema and full API: follow, unfollow, followers list, following list, activity feed","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","title":"Add skeleton loading to SpotlightSearch during semantic rerank","description":"# Task: Add Skeleton Loading to SpotlightSearch ## Context When semantic reranking is enabled, SpotlightSearch shows BM25 results immediately then reranks them. Currently, the only feedback is "Improving..." text in the footer. Users may not notice results are being reordered. ## Current Behavior 1. User types query 2. BM25 results appear immediately 3. Footer shows "Improving..." with sparkle icon 4. Results silently reorder when reranking completes ## Proposed Behavior 1. User types query 2. Skeleton placeholders appear with shimmer effect 3. BM25 results fade in (partial loading state) 4. "Improving..." indicator visible 5. Results smoothly reorder with animation 6. "Semantic" badge replaces "Improving..." ## Implementation ### 1. Skeleton Component for Search Results ```tsx function SearchResultSkeleton() { return (
); } ``` ### 2. Show Skeletons During Initial Search ```tsx // In the results rendering section: {isSearching && results.length === 0 && (
{Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, i) => ( ))}
)} ``` ### 3. Add Reorder Animation ```tsx // Wrap results in AnimatePresence with layout animation {results.map((result, index) => ( {/* Result button content */} ))} ``` ### 4. Shimmer Effect (Optional Enhancement) ```css @keyframes shimmer { 0% { background-position: -200% 0; } 100% { background-position: 200% 0; } } .skeleton-shimmer { background: linear-gradient( 90deg, oklch(0.95 0 0) 0%, oklch(0.90 0 0) 50%, oklch(0.95 0 0) 100% ); background-size: 200% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite; } ``` ## Design Rationale - Skeletons communicate "loading" better than spinners - Layout animations make reordering feel smooth, not jarring - Staggered entrance (delay: index * 0.02) creates professional feel - Shimmer effect adds polish (optional) ## Testing 1. Enable semantic mode 2. Type query - see skeletons while loading 3. Results appear with staggered animation 4. When reranking completes, results smoothly reorder 5. Disable semantic mode - results still stagger in 6. Test with prefers-reduced-motion: animations disabled ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx ## Dependencies - Requires Framer Motion (already included) - Should match PromptGrid skeleton pattern - Respects reduced motion preference","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:40:58.534780914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:35:51.996696147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:35:51.996696147Z","close_reason":"Skeleton loading and layout animations already implemented in SpotlightSearch","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-usn5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:43:30.625160247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-897","title":"Add error boundaries with fallback UI -- Details: Wrap search, prompt grid, detail modals with error boundaries. Show friendly error message + retry button. Log errors for debugging. Select multiple reported items - Bulk approve (dismiss reports) - Bulk remove (hide content) - Bulk warn users - Action summary before confirm - Audit log for all actions ## Actions Available - Approve (dismiss report) - Hide (soft delete content) - Delete (permanent, admin only) - Warn user (send warning email) - Suspend user (requires individual review) ## API Endpoints - POST /api/admin/moderation/bulk-action - body: { itemIds: [], action: 'approve' | 'hide' | 'warn', reason: string } ## UI - Checkbox selection in moderation queue - Bulk action toolbar - Action confirmation modal - Progress indicator for large batches ## Success Criteria - Efficient batch processing - Clear audit trail - Error handling per item - Permission checks per action ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:23.160279476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:23.160279476Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8ick","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:32.707308055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8ick","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:32.834143138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8n2","title":"Build MessageContent component for transcript display","description":"Part 10.4 of PLAN. Form displays correctly 8. Required fields validated 9. Category selection works 10. Submission succeeds 11. Confirmation shown 12. Email sent to support ### Ticket System 13. User can view tickets 14. Ticket history displays 15. Can reply to open ticket 16. Status updates shown ### Search 17. Help search returns results 18. No results shows suggestions 19. Authentication Flow - Device code flow (RFC 8628) explanation - Token storage and refresh - Error handling ### 2. API Endpoints - /api/cli/device-code - Initiate auth - /api/cli/token - Exchange code for token - /api/cli/sync - Sync library - /api/cli/collections - Manage collections ### 3. Local Cache Structure - ~/.jeffreysprompts/ directory layout - credentials.json format - library/ cache structure - Sync metadata ### 4. Prompt Format Compatibility - Free tier prompt format (TypeScript) - Premium prompt format (database) - Conversion between formats - SKILL.md export format ### 5. Offline Mode - What works offline - Cache invalidation - Conflict resolution ## Files to Create - docs/cli-premium-integration.md - docs/api-reference.md - Update README.md with premium features section ## Success Criteria - Clear documentation for contributors - API contracts documented - Cache format specified - Troubleshooting guide included ## Cross-Repo Integration - Premium device code flow: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1 - Premium verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm - Premium CLI auth page: jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0 - Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl - Premium token refresh/revoke: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve - Premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:31:23.094413840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:14:05.791773776Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:14:05.791773776Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive CLI-Premium integration docs (cli-premium-integration.md + api-reference.md)","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-akwo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:54:20.526620963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-bojj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:36.038383066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:12.029126352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:44:52.838533775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:29.926171438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:45.072648583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-reul","title":"E2E Tests: API Documentation Pages","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-nyk6. ## Task Create E2E tests for the API documentation pages. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Documentation Navigation** - Documentation accessible - Examples work correctly - Search is useful - Mobile friendly ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:49.064942573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:49.064942573Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-reul","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t524","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:34.890280264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-reul","title":"E2E Tests: API Documentation Pages","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-nyk6. ## Task Create E2E tests for the API documentation pages. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Documentation Navigation** - Log navigation events - Log search queries - Log copy actions - Screenshot key pages ## Files to Create - e2e/docs/navigation.spec.ts - e2e/docs/api-reference.spec.ts - e2e/docs/interactive.spec.ts ## Success Criteria - Documentation accessible - Examples work correctly - Search is useful - Mobile friendly ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletHill","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:49.064942573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T02:32:08.991337579Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-reul","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t524","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:34.890280264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rhvk","title":"E2E Tests: Network Resilience and Offline Mode","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2b44. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create E2E tests for network failure handling and offline mode. ## Why This Matters - Users have bad connections - Mobile users go offline - Must handle gracefully - No data loss ## Test Scenarios ### 1. Network Failure Handling ```typescript test('should show error message on network failure', async ({ page, context }) => { await page.goto('/dashboard'); // Simulate offline await context.setOffline(true); // Try to load more content await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Load More' }).click(); // Should show offline message await expect(page.getByText(/network.*error|offline/i)).toBeVisible(); }); test('should retry failed requests', async ({ page, context }) => { await page.goto('/dashboard'); // Intercept and fail first request let requestCount = 0; await page.route('**/api/prompts**', async (route) => { requestCount++; if (requestCount === 1) { await route.abort('connectionfailed'); } else { await route.continue(); } }); // Should auto-retry and succeed await page.reload(); await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-list')).toBeVisible(); expect(requestCount).toBe(2); }); ``` ### 2. Offline Indicator ```typescript test('should show offline indicator when disconnected', async ({ page, context }) => { await page.goto('/dashboard'); await context.setOffline(true); // Should show offline indicator await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).toBeVisible(); await context.setOffline(false); // Should hide indicator await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).not.toBeVisible(); }); ``` ### 3. Offline Data Access (PWA) ```typescript test('should serve cached prompts when offline', async ({ page, context }) => { // First, visit while online to cache await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id'); await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible(); // Go offline await context.setOffline(true); // Should still show cached content await page.reload(); await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible(); await expect(page.getByText(/offline.*cached/i)).toBeVisible(); }); test('should queue actions when offline', async ({ page, context }) => { await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id/edit'); await context.setOffline(true); // Make an edit await page.getByLabel('Title').fill('Updated Title'); await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save' }).click(); // Should show "queued" message await expect(page.getByText(/saved.*sync/i)).toBeVisible(); // Go back online await context.setOffline(false); // Should sync automatically await expect(page.getByText(/synced/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 }); }); ``` ### 4. Slow Network Handling ```typescript test('should show loading states on slow network', async ({ page }) => { // Simulate slow network const client = await page.context().newCDPSession(page); await client.send('Network.emulateNetworkConditions', { offline: false, downloadThroughput: 50 * 1024, // 50kb/s uploadThroughput: 50 * 1024, latency: 2000, // 2s latency }); await page.goto('/search'); await page.getByPlaceholder('Search').fill('test'); await page.keyboard.press('Enter'); // Should show loading skeleton await expect(page.getByTestId('search-skeleton')).toBeVisible(); }); test('should timeout and show message on very slow network', async ({ page }) => { // Simulate extremely slow network await page.route('**/api/**', async (route) => { await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 30000)); await route.continue(); }); await page.goto('/dashboard'); // Should show timeout message await expect(page.getByText(/taking.*long|timeout/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 }); }); ``` ### 5. Reconnection Handling ```typescript test('should automatically reconnect and refresh', async ({ page, context }) => { await page.goto('/dashboard'); await context.setOffline(true); await page.waitForTimeout(1000); await context.setOffline(false); // Should show reconnection message await expect(page.getByText(/back online|reconnected/i)).toBeVisible(); // Should refresh data await expect(page.getByTestId('last-updated')).toContainText(/just now/i); }); ``` ## Logging Requirements - Log network state changes - Log retry attempts - Log cache hits/misses - Screenshot error states ## Files to Create - e2e/network/failures.spec.ts - e2e/network/offline.spec.ts - e2e/network/slow.spec.ts - e2e/network/reconnection.spec.ts ## Success Criteria - Errors shown clearly - Retries work - Offline mode functions - Reconnection smooth ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:22.775310033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:37:21.634517522Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T20:37:21.634517522Z","close_reason":"Fixed offline banner state reset + added offline indicator test hook","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rhvk","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-2hhc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:30.049598593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ri69","title":"Fix touch target sizes to meet 44px minimum","description":"# Task: Fix Touch Target Sizes ## Context Apple and Google HIG recommend minimum 44x44px touch targets for comfortable interaction.
